In Andhra Pradesh, the state government fiercely battled an absolute, massive ecological catastrophe. ## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On May 7, 1945, the massive, incredibly brutal and devastating European Theatre of World War II essentially and formally concluded. German General Alfred Jodl officially signed the massive, absolute unconditional surrender documents of all German armed forces at the Allied headquarters in Reims, France. The massive, historic signing heavily marked the absolute, definitive collapse of the Third Reich, deeply ending a massive, horrific global conflict that had completely annihilated millions of lives and heavily shattered the entire European continent.

On May 7, 1915, a massive, incredibly catastrophic, and historically polarizing maritime disaster occurred during World War I. A German U-boat aggressively and deliberately torpedoed the massive British ocean liner RMS *Lusitania* off the coast of Ireland. The incredibly swift, massive sinking of the civilian vessel resulted in the horrific deaths of 1,198 passengers, including 128 Americans. The massive outrage sparked by this attack aggressively shifted American public opinion, deeply serving as a massive, foundational catalyst for the US eventual entry into the war.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On May 7, 1861, Rabindranath Tagore, the absolute, towering, and universally revered genius of Indian literature and culture, was born in Calcutta. An incredibly prolific massive poet, philosopher, playwright, and composer, he heavily revolutionized Bengali literature. He achieved massive historical immortality by becoming the very first non-European to win the Nobel Prize in Literature in 1913. His incredibly profound, massive compositions were later heavily adopted as the official National Anthems of both India and Bangladesh.

On May 7, 1924, Alluri Sitarama Raju, the massive, incredibly fierce, and deeply heroic leader of the Rampa Rebellion, was brutally captured and aggressively executed by the British colonial police. He aggressively led massive, highly coordinated armed guerrilla campaigns utilizing indigenous tribal forces against oppressive British forestry laws in the Madras Presidency. His massive, legendary resistance heavily elevated him to the status of an absolute, mythical folk hero and a massive martyr in the incredibly brutal struggle for Indian independence.
